text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name: Renata Kowalczyk
Medical Record Number: [MRN on file]
Accession Number: HP-[ACCESSION ON FILE]
Ordering Physician: Hematology/Oncology (Bone Marrow Transplant Program)
Clinical Indication: AML post-alloHSCT (10/10 MUD), day +365 restaging and disease reassessment; history of FLT3-ITD (high allelic ratio) AML, FAB M4, ELN Adverse risk, now in complete remission following myeloablative conditioning (FluBu4) and peripheral blood stem cell transplant from 38-year-old male donor.
Specimen Type: Posterior iliac crest bone marrow core biopsy and aspirate smears
Procedure: Bilateral posterior iliac crest bone marrow biopsy and aspirate (right side performed; left side obtained for bilateral sampling)
Primary Site Sampled: Right posterior iliac crest (core biopsy and aspirate); Left posterior iliac crest (additional core for cytogenetics/chimerism)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CLINICAL HISTORY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This is a 49-year-old female with a known history of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), initially diagnosed with a presenting WBC of 87,400/microliter, bone marrow blast count of 74%, FLT3-ITD mutation (VAF 0.82, insertion 48 bp, high allelic ratio), and DNMT3A R882H co-mutation on a background of normal karyotype (46,XX). ELN 2022 risk classification: Adverse. She achieved complete morphologic remission (CR1) after one cycle of induction chemotherapy with 7+3 plus midostaurin; day +28 bone marrow biopsy demonstrated MRD-negative CR by multiparameter flow cytometry (8-color panel, sensitivity 0.01%) and FLT3-ITD PCR. She subsequently received one cycle of bridging consolidation with intermediate-dose cytarabine (IDAC) followed by myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (alloHSCT) from a 10/10 allele-level HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD; 38-year-old CMV-negative male), conditioned with fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4) and ATG; GVHD prophylaxis with tacrolimus/short-course Trexall. Post-transplant course notable for mild SOS/VOD (resolved without defibrotide), CMV viremia (treated with valganciclovir, cleared), and mild chronic GVHD (skin lichenoid, sicca syndrome; managed topically). Prior bone marrow assessments at day +28, +60 (MRD negative, 99% donor chimerism by STR; 46,XY on cytogenetics confirming male donor engraftment) and most recent peripheral blood chimerism at day +150 (100% donor). This biopsy is the protocol-mandated 12-month (day +365) post-transplant disease reassessment.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Right Posterior Iliac Crest:
Core biopsy: Received in 10% neutral buffered formalin, labeled with patient name and MRN. One core of pale tan to red-brown bone marrow tissue measuring 2.1 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is intact, firm, and suitable for histologic processing. Submitted in entirety for decalcification (EDTA), paraffin embedding, and serial sectioning.

Aspirate: Received as 6 direct smears and 2 concentrate (buffy coat) smears, Wright-Giemsa stained. Smears are cellular with grossly visible spicules on the direct preparation. Additional aspirate material submitted for flow cytometry immunophenotyping, cytogenetic analysis (conventional karyotype and FISH), molecular studies (FLT3-ITD PCR, DNMT3A R882H ddPCR), and chimerism analysis by STR.

Left Posterior Iliac Crest:
Core biopsy: One core of pale tan bone marrow tissue measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. Submitted for decalcification and histologic processing, reserved in part for touch preparations.

Touch preparations: 4 air-dried touch preparation slides prepared from the core biopsy at time of procedure; submitted for Wright-Giemsa staining.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

I. MORPHOLOGY (CORE BIOPSY — HEMATOXYLIN AND EOSIN, RETICULIN, PAS STAINS)

Cellularity:
The bone marrow core biopsy demonstrates normocellular marrow with an estimated cellularity of 40-50%, appropriate for the patient's age (49 years) and post-transplant status (day +365 following myeloablative conditioning). Cellularity is evenly distributed throughout the core without focal areas of hypocellularity or fibrosis.

Hematopoiesis:
Trilineage hematopoiesis is present and morphologically normal:

- Erythroid series: Normoblastic erythropoiesis with maturing erythroid precursors in well-formed erythroid islands. Nuclear-cytoplasmic maturation synchrony is appropriate. No megaloblastic change. No dysplastic features (no nuclear budding, no internuclear bridging, no ringed sideroblasts on iron stain).

- Myeloid series: Orderly granulocytic maturation sequence present from myeloblasts through mature segmented neutrophils. Myeloblasts are rare and do not form clusters or sheets. No abnormal granulation. No pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly. No dysgranulopoiesis.

- Megakaryocytic series: Megakaryocytes are present in normal numbers (approximately 3-5 per high-power field in active areas), distributed evenly throughout the marrow. Megakaryocytes are predominantly mature, with appropriate lobulation and abundant cytoplasm. No micromegakaryocytes. No significant hypolobation. No emperipolesis.

Blast Count:
Morphologic differential count performed on aspirate smears (500-cell differential). Myeloblasts: <2% (1.8% by differential count), within normal limits. No monocytic blasts or promonocytes identified. No Auer rods identified.

Monocytic Component:
The monocyte/promonocyte population is not increased. No abnormal monocytic forms. Mature monocytes account for <5% of the differential.

Stroma and Vasculature:
Reticulin stain (Gordon-Sweet silver impregnation): Grade 0-1 reticulin fibrosis (minimal; fine fibers without intersections). No increased reticulin. No collagen fibrosis (trichrome stain negative for collagen). Sinusoidal architecture is intact. No evidence of necrosis, granulomas, or fat necrosis. Lymphoid aggregates: A small, well-circumscribed, non-paratrabecular lymphoid aggregate noted (1 focus, <50 lymphocytes, predominantly small, mature-appearing lymphocytes); this finding is non-specific in the post-transplant setting and may represent residual reactive lymphoid tissue. No abnormal lymphoid infiltrates.

Iron Stores:
Prussian Blue (Perls) iron stain: Iron stores are present and adequate (grade 2/4 storage iron). No ringed sideroblasts identified. No hemosiderin-laden macrophages in excess.

Osseous Trabeculae:
Bony trabeculae show normal thickness and architecture. No evidence of osteonecrosis. No trabecular thickening or remodeling beyond expected post-conditioning changes.

Conclusion, Morphology: No morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia. No evidence of myelodysplasia. Trilineage hematopoiesis with normal maturation. Normocellular marrow appropriate for post-transplant day +365.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
II. IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
The following immunohistochemical stains were performed on formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded core biopsy sections:

- CD34 (QBEnd/10): Rare scattered positivity in endothelial cells and rare hematopoietic progenitors only; no increase in CD34+ blasts. No clusters or sheets of CD34+ cells. Estimated CD34+ blasts: <1% of marrow cells.

- CD117 (c-KIT): Scattered positivity in early myeloid precursors and mast cells only; no abnormal clustering or increased staining.

- MPO (Myeloperoxidase): Highlights mature granulocytic elements with appropriate progressive cytoplasmic staining; no aberrant staining pattern.

- CD64: Scattered monocytic cells highlighted; no increase. No aberrant blast-associated expression.

- CD20: Highlights rare scattered B-cells; no B-cell aggregate with concerning features for post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD). The small lymphoid aggregate highlighted predominantly as CD3+ T-cells on additional staining.

- CD3: Highlights T-cells in the small aggregate and scattered interstitial T-lymphocytes; distribution appropriate for post-transplant reconstitution.

- CD138: Scattered plasma cells present, <2% of cellularity; no abnormal plasma cell infiltrate.

Interpretation: No immunohistochemical evidence of AML recurrence. No CD34+/CD117+ blast expansion. No features of post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

A. FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING — MRD ANALYSIS
(Performed on aspirate, 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry, high-sensitivity panel for AML MRD; sensitivity 0.01%)

Result: NEGATIVE for immunophenotypically aberrant myeloid blasts.

Cells analyzed: 500,000 viable events acquired.
Gating strategy: CD45/SSC gating with lymphocyte, monocyte, and blast gates verified.
Blast gate (CD45dim, low SSC): <0.01% of viable cells; phenotypically normal, no aberrant co-expression of CD34+/CD7, CD34+/CD56, CD34+/CD11b, or CD117+/CD64 detected.
Mature myeloid elements: Normal granulocytic and monocytic maturation pattern without left shift or aberrant antigen expression.
Erythroid precursors: Present in normal proportions with appropriate maturation.
T-cell and B-cell precursor populations: Present; B-cell precursors (hematogones) present in mildly increased numbers (3.2%), consistent with post-transplant immune reconstitution (expected finding at 12 months post-transplant).
NK cells: Present; no aberrant NK cell population.

Summary: MRD NEGATIVE. No evidence of residual or recurrent AML by high-sensitivity flow cytometry.

B. MOLECULAR STUDIES

1. FLT3-ITD Detection by Fragment Analysis/PCR:
Result: UNDETECTABLE
Method: Capillary electrophoresis-based fragment analysis with sensitivity of 1 in 10,000 (10^-4). No FLT3-ITD peak detected in the diagnostic size range (48 bp insertion). Wild-type FLT3 peak present, confirming sample adequacy. Assay performed on bone marrow aspirate DNA.
Interpretation: FLT3-ITD undetectable, consistent with sustained molecular remission.

2. DNMT3A p.R882H (c.2644C>T) by Droplet Digital PCR (ddPCR):
Result: NOT DETECTED (below limit of detection)
Method: ddPCR allele-specific assay; limit of detection <0.05% VAF.
Prior results for comparison: VAF 47% at diagnosis; VAF 1.8% at day +28 (post-induction); VAF 2.1% at day +60 (post-transplant, attributed to CHIP within residual host hematopoiesis).
Interpretation: DNMT3A R882H now undetectable, consistent with 100% donor engraftment supplanting any residual host hematopoiesis. Prior low-level signal (VAF ~2%) at day +60 was interpreted as CHIP within the small residual host cell fraction; its disappearance at day +365 is consistent with complete replacement by donor-derived hematopoiesis.

3. Additional Molecular Assays (Reflex Panel, Pending at Time of Finalization - results to be reported as addendum):
- Comprehensive myeloid next-generation sequencing panel (reflex, per protocol): PENDING
- WT1 quantitative PCR (MRD surrogate): PENDING
Addendum will be issued upon receipt of pending results.

C. CYTOGENETICS

1. Conventional Karyotype (G-banding):
Result: 46,XY [20/20]
Interpretation: All 20 metaphases analyzed show a normal male karyotype. This finding confirms complete donor engraftment (patient is female; donor is male), with no residual host metaphases detected. At diagnosis, the patient's karyotype was 46,XX; conversion to 46,XY is consistent with complete replacement by the male allogeneic donor's hematopoiesis. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities identified in the donor-derived cells.

2. FISH Panel (AML-Targeted):
Result: NEGATIVE for all probes tested.
- RUNX1-RUNX1T1 [t(8;21)]: Negative
- CBFB-MYH11 [inv(16)/t(16;16)]: Negative
- PML-RARA [t(15;17)]: Negative
- KMT2A (MLL) rearrangement (11q23): Negative
- FLT3 locus (13q12) deletion/rearrangement: No deletion
- MYC (8q24) amplification: Negative
- TP53 (17p13) deletion: Negative
Interpretation: No FISH-detectable cytogenetic abnormalities.

D. CHIMERISM ANALYSIS BY SHORT TANDEM REPEAT (STR)

1. Peripheral Blood (whole blood, obtained same date as bone marrow procedure):
Result: 100% DONOR CHIMERISM (no host alleles detected in informative STR loci)

2. Bone Marrow (CD34+ progenitor-enriched sorted fraction):
Result: 100% DONOR CHIMERISM
Method: PCR-based STR analysis using 16 informative polymorphic loci; donor and recipient pre-transplant baseline STR profiles on file for comparison.
Sensitivity: Able to detect host cells at ≥1-2% of total.
Interpretation: Complete donor engraftment in both total peripheral blood and CD34+ progenitor compartments. No evidence of host hematopoietic reconstitution.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FINAL DIAGNOSIS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST BONE MARROW BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE (DAY +365 POST-ALLOHSCT):

1. NO MORPHOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF RESIDUAL OR RECURRENT ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA (AML). Blast count <2% by morphologic differential.

2. NORMAL TRILINEAGE HEMATOPOIESIS with normocellular marrow (40-50% cellularity), appropriate for age and post-transplant status.

3. COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC AND MOLECULAR REMISSION (CR/MRD-negative):
   - Flow cytometry MRD: Negative (sensitivity 0.01%)
   - FLT3-ITD PCR: Undetectable (sensitivity 10^-4)
   - DNMT3A R882H ddPCR: Not detected (below assay LOD)

4. COMPLETE DONOR ENGRAFTMENT CONFIRMED:
   - Conventional karyotype: 46,XY [20/20] (male karyotype consistent with male donor; patient is female)
   - STR chimerism (peripheral blood and CD34+ sorted fraction): 100% donor

5. MINIMAL RETICULIN FIBROSIS (Grade 0-1), no collagen fibrosis.

6. SMALL REACTIVE LYMPHOID AGGREGATE, non-paratrabecular; non-specific in post-transplant setting; no features of post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD).

7. MILDLY INCREASED HEMATOGONES (3.2% by flow cytometry), consistent with normal post-transplant B-cell immune reconstitution.

8. ANCILLARY STUDIES PENDING: Comprehensive myeloid NGS panel and WT1 quantitative PCR results pending; addendum to follow.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This day +365 bone marrow evaluation demonstrates sustained complete morphologic and molecular remission in a patient with previously high-risk AML (FLT3-ITD, high allelic ratio; ELN Adverse) following myeloablative allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor. Complete donor chimerism is confirmed by both conventional cytogenetics (46,XY replacing the patient's prior 46,XX karyotype) and STR analysis (100% donor in peripheral blood and CD34+ progenitor fractions). The disappearance of the previously detected low-level DNMT3A R882H signal (VAF ~2% at day +60, attributed to pre-existing CHIP within residual host hematopoiesis) is consistent with complete replacement of host hematopoiesis by the donor graft. FLT3-ITD remains undetectable at the 10^-4 sensitivity threshold of the fragment analysis assay. The overall findings at one year post-transplant are highly favorable and consistent with durable disease control. The small non-paratrabecular lymphoid aggregate and mildly increased hematogones are expected post-transplant immune reconstitution findings and do not suggest PTLD or lymphoma; clinical correlation is recommended if interval changes occur.

Results communicated to the treating hematology-oncology/BMT team.
Pending addendum to be issued upon receipt of NGS panel and WT1 PCR results.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SIGNATURE
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Interpreting Pathologist: [Hematopathologist, MD, PhD — Signature on file]
Department of Hematopathology
Affiliating Academic Medical Center

Electronic signature applied upon finalization.
Report status: FINAL (Addendum pending for NGS/WT1 results)
